story: It starts with a radio squawk—“Code 6 in Housewares”—and no one can define it. Grapes are sampled, towels become shields, and a panicked shopper’s lawful handgun pops two rounds into a towel stack; no injuries. Witnesses disagree on everything; EMTs argue Doritos. Housewares is stable; I find no emergency and start telling departments police aren’t required. In the back office, a teen temp, pale, sets down the store radio after blurting “Code 6” as a dare. Shoppers now want to keep the “miracle towels.” I’m hungry and staring at paperwork.
implausible_knowledge: In the back office, a teen temp, pale, sets down the store radio after blurting “Code 6” as a dare.
question: As the officer closes out the call after finding no actual emergency, what will the officer do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Go to the back office to detain and question the teen who made the “Code 6” broadcast, opening a false-report investigation.
answer_without_knowledge: Finish the report and clear the scene, leaving internal radio issues to store management.
